response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned software engineer with strong systems and full-stack breadth, but the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial-automation and IEC 62443 security experience. His only professional C++ work was a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015; the past decade has been Node.js/TypeScript and DLang web/backend work. There is no evidence of message brokers at scale, MQTT/IoT protocols, SDL practice, or industrial cybersecurity, making the fit for a Senior Manager C++ position weak despite his overall seniority.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering-craft background: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source authorship
15+ years of broad experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — brief role in 2014-2015, none since
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or MQTT/OPC-UA/Modbus protocol exposure
Recent stack (TypeScript/Node, AI tooling) diverges from the role's core C++ and security focus

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
Proven high-performance systems and mentoring capability
